name: Manteigas and Zêzere Valley Loop
name_original: Giro ad anello da Manteigas verso Sameiro
description: Challenging 21.7-mile mountain bike loop from Manteigas to Sameiro in Serra da Estrela, with 3162 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Manteigas (São Pedro) offers diverse and challenging terrain within Portugal's Serra da Estrela Natural Park. The region is characterized by high peaks, forest-covered slopes, and the magnificent U-shaped Zêzere Glacier Valley. Riders can expect a mix of paved roads, fire roads, and singletrack, providing varied experiences across its natural landscapes. Best mountain bike trails around Manteigas (São Pedro) The most popular mountain bike trail is Serra da Estrela: Manteigas and Torre Loop, a 37.8 miles (60.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers rugged terrain and panoramic views towards Portugal's highest mountain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Great Zêzere Route, a difficult 29.4 miles (47.3 km) path. This route explores the dramatic Zêzere Glacier Valley, known for its unique glacial features. Local mountain bikers also love the Loop from Manteigas to Covão da Ponte – Serra da Estrela Natural Park, a 29.4 miles (47.3 km) trail leading through the Serra da Estrela Natural Park, often completed in about 5 hours 1 minute. Mountain biking around Manteigas (São Pedro) is defined by glacial valleys, high peaks, and forest-covered slopes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Manteigas (São Pedro) are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Manteigas (São Pedro)'s varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: Manteigas (São Pedro) offers a selection of over 25 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult, and are highly rated by the komoot community.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The mountain bike trails in Manteigas (São Pedro) range from moderate to difficult. There are 8 moderate trails and 16 difficult trails, providing options for different experience levels. The region's terrain, characterized by high peaks and glacial valleys, naturally lends itself to more challenging rides.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: While many trails in Manteigas (São Pedro) are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available. The region's BTT center also offers a range of trails, from green to black, suggesting options for less experienced riders, though the overall terrain is quite varied and can be demanding.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: You can expect to ride through diverse and stunning landscapes. The region is defined by the magnificent U-shaped Zêzere Glacier Valley, high peaks, and forest-covered slopes. Trails often feature a mix of paved roads, fire roads, and singletrack, offering panoramic views, especially towards Portugal's highest mountain, Torre, and areas like Penhas Douradas.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer access to stunning natural features. You can explore the dramatic Zêzere Glacier Valley, visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6019826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hidden cascade at Poço do Inferno&lt;/a&gt;, or ride towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1258525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre (Serra da Estrela Summit)&lt;/a&gt; for expansive views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4915608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake&lt;/a&gt; (Lagoa Comprida) and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3477370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale do Rossim Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; are also prominent features in the area.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that are loop trails?
A: Yes, Manteigas (São Pedro) features several excellent loop trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330365737/serra-da-estrela-manteigas-and-torre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra da Estrela: Manteigas and Torre Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330306349/loop-from-manteigas-to-covao-da-ponte-serra-da-estrela-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop from Manteigas to Covão da Ponte – Serra da Estrela Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330590394/manteigas-and-zezere-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manteigas and Zêzere Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse and challenging terrain, the stunning natural landscapes of the Serra da Estrela Natural Park, and the varied riding experience that includes paved roads, fire roads, and singletrack.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the Zêzere Glacier Valley?
A: Absolutely. The Zêzere Glacier Valley is a highlight of the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330285159/great-zezere-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great Zêzere Route&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to explore this dramatic U-shaped valley, known for its unique glacial features. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330590394/manteigas-and-zezere-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manteigas and Zêzere Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find wild swimming spots near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region offers opportunities for wild swimming. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4915608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake&lt;/a&gt; (Lagoa Comprida) is the largest water reservoir in the Serra da Estrela and a glacial lake, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3477370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale do Rossim Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; also provides options. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6019826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hidden cascade at Poço do Inferno&lt;/a&gt;, though primarily a waterfall, is located in a steep gorge and can be a refreshing spot.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The Serra da Estrela region, where Manteigas is located, experiences distinct seasons. Spring and autumn are generally ideal for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. In spring, waterfalls like Poço do Inferno are particularly impressive, while autumn brings stunning foliage, especially at the Beeches of São Lourenço. Summers can be warm, and winters can be cold with snow at higher elevations, potentially freezing waterfalls.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that lead to high mountain peaks or viewpoints?
A: Definitely. The region is known for its high peaks and panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330365737/serra-da-estrela-manteigas-and-torre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra da Estrela: Manteigas and Torre Loop&lt;/a&gt; involves a challenging ascent towards Portugal's highest mountain, Torre, offering rugged terrain and expansive vistas. Many routes also pass through areas like Penhas Douradas, which are known for their stunning landscapes and viewpoints.
tips: Here you ride on a wide dirt path that winds its way through the thick vegetation of the forest. The scents are wonderful and the shade gives you a bit of relief even on the hottest days.
In this pretty village immersed in the quiet of the surrounding nature you can stop for a regenerating break.

It is considered the "capital of Serra da Estrela" and a privileged starting point to explore the Serra da Estrela Natural Park. 

Manteigas is famous for its wool textile industry, especially for the production of fabrics and blankets. Manteigas is also known for its natural beauty, with glacial valleys (such as Zêzere), waterfalls and unique rock formations, including the famous Cântaro Magro.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Loop from Manteigas towards Sameiro trail?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike trail is rated as difficult. It demands very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Riders should be prepared for challenging terrain, including sections where pushing your bike might be necessary, with technical difficulty aligning with Single Track Scale (STS) S3-S6.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied, offering a mix of paved sections, fire roads, and challenging single tracks. You'll encounter steep inclines and declines, as well as some difficult double tracks. The route traverses diverse landscapes including bush mosaics, open meadows, streams, dense forest areas, and agricultural land.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Loop from Manteigas towards Sameiro?
faqs.answer: Beyond the natural beauty of the Trail in the forest, you'll pass through the charming settlement of Sameiro and start/end in Manteigas. The trail also features traditional schist and granite houses, various popular and religious monuments like the St. John Baptist Church and St. Lawrence Chapel, and picturesque views of the Bear Valley Stream.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Rota do Maciço Central, Grande Rota do Zêzere, Grande Rota das Aldeias Históricas - Variante, and Rota do Sol, among others.

faqs.question: Is the Loop from Manteigas towards Sameiro trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Serra da Estrela Natural Park, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the challenging terrain and potential for wildlife encounters on this specific mountain biking route. Ensure your dog has the fitness for a difficult 35 km ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this trail is typically during spring, early summer, or autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures and generally stable weather conditions. Winter can bring snow and ice, making the already difficult terrain even more challenging and potentially impassable.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access the Serra da Estrela Natural Park or this trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific entrance fees or permits required to access the Serra da Estrela Natural Park or to ride this particular trail. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning nature conservation and private property.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Manteigas. You can typically find parking within the town, which serves as a convenient base for accessing the trail. Specific trailhead parking spots may vary, so it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What kind of natural beauty and flora can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail offers stunning natural beauty with panoramic vistas of agricultural fields, winding water lines, lush pastures, and dense forests. You'll encounter diverse flora including willows, heather, gum rockrose, broom, rosemary, ash, alder, wild pine, Douglas-fir, and holm-oak, providing a vibrant and ever-changing landscape.

faqs.question: Are there any alternatives for less experienced mountain bikers in the Manteigas area?
faqs.answer: Given that this loop is rated as difficult and requires advanced skills, less experienced mountain bikers might want to explore other, shorter, or less technical routes around Manteigas. Researching trails specifically rated as 'moderate' or 'easy' on komoot or local tourism sites would be a good starting point to find suitable alternatives.

faqs.question: What makes this trail unique within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park?
faqs.answer: This trail offers more than just a mountain biking challenge; it provides an immersive journey into the essence of the mountain people and their history. Riders can connect with the region's heritage through its traditional villages, historical monuments, and structures like community ovens, all set within the stunning natural backdrop of the Serra da Estrela Natural Park.
